title: Break the critic-deep ↔ decomposer-deep import cycle via dependency injection
description: Fully eliminate the genuine runtime import cycle between src/fleet/critic-deep.ts and src/fleet/decomposer-deep.ts. Inject runExpandStage into runCritiqueLoop as a function parameter so critic-deep no longer VALUE-imports decomposer-deep; relocate the `Outline` type (decomposer-deep.ts:86, sole external importer critic-deep.ts) into a new dependency-free leaf so critic-deep no longer TYPE-imports decomposer-deep either. After this, `grep decomposer-deep src/fleet/critic-deep.ts` returns nothing and only a one-directional decomposer-deep → critic-deep edge remains. decomposeGoalDeep (the SOLE production caller of runCritiqueLoop, decomposer-deep.ts:367) passes its in-file runExpandStage into the loop; its exported signature is unchanged. Pure structural refactor, zero behavior change.

successCriteria.description: Build + typecheck pass with zero errors (npm run build, npm run typecheck).

critic-deep.ts has NO import from the cyclic module ./decomposer-deep.js (neither value nor type): `grep -nE 'from "\./decomposer-deep\.js"' src/fleet/critic-deep.ts` returns ZERO matches. Imports from the dependency-free leaves ./decomposer-deep-types.js and ./decomposer-deep-constants.js ARE expected and fine (they contain the substring 'decomposer-deep' but are not the cyclic module). decomposer-deep.ts may still import runCritiqueLoop from critic-deep (one direction is not a cycle).

runCritiqueLoop's input object gains an injected re-expand function (e.g. `expand: typeof runExpandStage` or a narrower function type); critic-deep.ts's former runExpandStage call (was :247) now invokes the injected function. decomposeGoalDeep (decomposer-deep.ts:367) passes its local runExpandStage in. decomposeGoalDeep's EXPORTED signature is byte-unchanged.

The `Outline` type lives in a new dependency-free leaf (e.g. src/fleet/decomposer-deep-types.ts with ZERO relative imports). decomposer-deep.ts re-exports Outline (so its public surface is unchanged); critic-deep imports Outline from the leaf. The leaf imports nothing from critic-deep or decomposer-deep.

Full vitest suite passes; the 9 runCritiqueLoop calls in critic-deep.test.ts (lines ~359-476) and any affected decomposer-deep.test.ts cases are updated to supply the injected re-expand function (a stub/the real runExpandStage). Only the 6 known cockpit-integration MCP failures may remain.

lint passes (zero errors); ESM .js specifiers, `import type` for the relocated Outline, unicode section headers preserved. The diff is confined to src/fleet/critic-deep.ts, decomposer-deep.ts, the new types leaf, critic-deep.test.ts, and (if touched) decomposer-deep.test.ts.

No behavior change: the critique→re-expand→accept-best loop and its never-throw/accept-on-exhaustion semantics are identical; only the source of runExpandStage (injected vs imported) and the Outline import path change. A test confirms runCritiqueLoop still re-expands by asserting the injected function is invoked on a critique miss.

nonGoals: Do NOT touch Cycle 1 (orchestrator/memory/fact-judge ↔ reconcile) — it is type-only and benign.
Do NOT change critique/expand budget constants, retry counts, early-stop, or accept-best logic.
Do NOT alter decomposeGoalDeep's public signature or runPlanStage.
Do NOT add logging, history events, or CLI surface.
Do NOT reverse the injection direction (do NOT inject runCritiqueLoop into decomposer-deep) — that would change decomposeGoalDeep's public signature and touch more call sites.
Do NOT delete decomposer-deep's export of Outline (re-export it from the leaf for backward compat).
stopConditions: critic-deep.ts has zero imports from decomposer-deep.ts (value or type), the cycle is gone, decomposeGoalDeep's signature is unchanged, and build/typecheck/test/lint are green
definitionOfDone: runCritiqueLoop accepts an injected re-expand function and calls it instead of an imported runExpandStage; critic-deep.ts no longer imports decomposer-deep.ts at all (the value import is removed, and `Outline` is imported from a new dependency-free leaf that decomposer-deep also re-exports); decomposeGoalDeep passes its local runExpandStage into runCritiqueLoop at decomposer-deep.ts:367 with no signature change; critic-deep.test.ts and any affected decomposer-deep.test.ts cases supply the injected function; build/typecheck/test/lint green.
assumptions: critic-deep.ts:4 is `import { type Outline, runExpandStage } from "./decomposer-deep.js"` — runExpandStage is the runtime value to remove; Outline is the type to re-source from a leaf.
runCritiqueLoop is defined at critic-deep.ts:208 and calls runExpandStage once (was :247) inside the re-expand branch.

decomposeGoalDeep (decomposer-deep.ts:337) calls runExpandStage at :358 (local) and runCritiqueLoop at :367 — it is the ONLY production caller of runCritiqueLoop (grep-verified). It already has runExpandStage in scope to pass in.

Outline is `export type Outline = { areas: OutlineArea[] }` at decomposer-deep.ts:86; its only out-of-file importer is critic-deep.ts (grep-verified), so relocation is low-risk. OutlineArea may need to move with it (verify and co-locate in the leaf).
decomposer-deep-constants.ts is the existing dependency-free-leaf precedent (consts only); a sibling decomposer-deep-types.ts keeps the constants file honest.

critic-deep.test.ts imports runCritiqueLoop (line 14) and calls it 9 times (359,379,400,418,433,447,461,476); these calls must now include the injected re-expand arg. The simplest test edit injects a small stub that returns a valid manifest (or re-imports the real runExpandStage).
outOfScope: Fleet behavior, budget constants, early-stop/accept-best logic
generatorNotes:
Read FIRST in full: src/fleet/critic-deep.ts (runCritiqueLoop :208 + the runExpandStage call ~ :247), src/fleet/decomposer-deep.ts (Outline :86, runExpandStage :295, decomposeGoalDeep :337-367), src/fleet/decomposer-deep-constants.ts (the leaf pattern), src/fleet/critic-deep.test.ts (runCritiqueLoop describe block ~ :354-480) and src/fleet/decomposer-deep.test.ts.

STEP 1 — new leaf src/fleet/decomposer-deep-types.ts: move `export type Outline = { areas: OutlineArea[] }` here, plus `OutlineArea` (and any types Outline depends on). The leaf must have ZERO relative imports (mirror decomposer-deep-constants.ts). Add a unicode section header + a `// bober:` comment noting it exists to break the critic-deep↔decomposer-deep cycle.

STEP 2 — decomposer-deep.ts: replace the local Outline/OutlineArea definitions with `export { type Outline, type OutlineArea } from "./decomposer-deep-types.js"` (re-export so the public surface and all existing `import { Outline } from ./decomposer-deep.js` paths keep working). Keep `import { runCritiqueLoop } from "./critic-deep.js"`. At :367, change the call to pass the injected fn: `return runCritiqueLoop({ client, model, goal, outline, baseline: manifest, expandMaxRetries, expand: runExpandStage });` (use whatever param name you pick in STEP 3; runExpandStage is already in-file).

STEP 3 — critic-deep.ts: DELETE `runExpandStage` from the decomposer-deep import. Change line 4 to import Outline from the leaf: `import type { Outline } from "./decomposer-deep-types.js"`. Add an injected param to runCritiqueLoop's input object — type it precisely, e.g. `expand: (input: { client: LLMClient; model: string; goal: string; outline: Outline; expandMaxRetries: number }) => Promise<FleetManifest>` (match runExpandStage's real signature — read it at decomposer-deep.ts:295). At the former :247 call site, call `input.expand({...})` instead of `runExpandStage({...})`. After this, `grep decomposer-deep src/fleet/critic-deep.ts` MUST be empty.

STEP 4 — tests: in critic-deep.test.ts add the injected `expand` to every runCritiqueLoop call. Cleanest: define one stub near the top of the describe block (e.g. `const fakeExpand = async () => baselineManifest` or re-import the real runExpandStage and pass it) and spread it into each call's input. For the re-expand assertion (sc-1-7), use a spy stub and assert it was called when the critic verdict misses. Update decomposer-deep.test.ts only if it relied on the old Outline import path or the runCritiqueLoop signature.

Follow ESM `.js` imports, `import type`, no synchronous fs, unicode section headers. Commit: `bober(sprint-1): break critic-deep<->decomposer-deep cycle via runExpandStage injection`.
evaluatorNotes: sc-1-2 (CRITICAL): run `grep -nE 'decomposer-deep' src/fleet/critic-deep.ts` — MUST be empty (no value import, no `import type { Outline } from ./decomposer-deep.js`). Then confirm decomposer-deep.ts still imports runCritiqueLoop (one direction only). sc-1-4: open the new leaf and run `grep -cE "from ['\"]\." src/fleet/decomposer-deep-types.ts` → expect 0 (dependency-free); confirm decomposer-deep re-exports Outline so external `import { Outline } from ./decomposer-deep.js` still resolves (typecheck proves it). sc-1-3: read runCritiqueLoop's signature — confirm an injected fn param exists and the body calls it (not an imported runExpandStage); confirm decomposeGoalDeep passes runExpandStage in and its OWN export signature is unchanged (diff the `export async function decomposeGoalDeep(` line). sc-1-7: confirm the re-expand branch still runs by reading the test that spies the injected fn. sc-1-5: full vitest, only the 6 known cockpit failures; the 9 runCritiqueLoop test calls compile and pass with the new arg. Build/typecheck/lint clean. git diff --stat should list only critic-deep.ts, decomposer-deep.ts, decomposer-deep-types.ts (new), critic-deep.test.ts, and optionally decomposer-deep.test.ts. OPTIONAL bonus: if tokensave or madge is available, confirm the fleet cycle no longer appears.
